source.title: Narcolepsy - clinical spectrum, aetiopathophysiology, diagnosis and treatment.

confidence_rationale: Authoritative consensus review on narcolepsy as the human knockout of orexin signalling. The clinical phenotype -- excessive daytime sleepiness PLUS cataplexy (loss of postural tone triggered by emotion) PLUS broader disturbances in motor, psychiatric, emotional, cognitive, metabolic, and autonomic functions -- is exactly what the loss of a single broadcast gain-modulator predicts. Multiple-system failure from a single neuropeptide loss is the negative-evidence equivalent of demonstrating broadcast architecture. Confidence high because the clinical presentation is well-replicated across cohorts and the orexin-deficiency mechanism is biochemically confirmed (CSF orexin levels diagnostic).
failure_signatures: Cataplexy (loss of motor tone triggered by strong emotion) is a specific failure of motor-emotion coupling -- exactly the kind of integration failure the broadcast loss predicts.
Narcolepsy patients show altered metabolic regulation despite normal endocrine function, suggesting orexin's metabolic-arousal coupling is functionally important even outside sleep.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Narcolepsy type 1 reflects selective loss of LHA orexin neurons. The phenotype is multi-system: excessive daytime sleepiness, cataplexy, sleep-wake fragmentation, hallucinations, sleep paralysis, but ALSO disturbances of motor, psychiatric, emotional, cognitive, metabolic, and autonomic function. The breadth of involvement reflects loss of a small but broadly-projecting gain-modulator.
mapping.ree_translation: Narcolepsy is the human existence proof for what the spec hypothesises -- a single broadcast neuromodulator whose loss produces multi-system failure rather than a focal deficit. For REE, this is strong negative evidence supporting broadcast over multi-target competition: if orexin were merely one of many redundant point-to-point signals, its loss would produce focal symptoms rather than system-wide dysregulation. Cataplexy specifically -- loss of postural tone triggered by emotion -- is a clean prediction of broadcast-coupling failure: emotional input no longer recruits the motor-arousal coupling, so the motor system collapses while emotion proceeds.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Narcolepsy is the loss of orexin specifically, not the loss of a generic broadcast override. Other broadcast neuromodulators (5-HT, NE, DA, ACh) intact. The clinical phenotype depends on what those other systems can compensate for and what they cannot. Translating this to REE means: SD-036's GABAergic decay and the proposed orexin-analog override are likely partially redundant in normal function but mechanistically distinct, and the SD-036-only fix to V3-EXQ-471 may be insufficient under stronger threat lock.
mapping.source_context: Consensus review of narcolepsy clinical features, mechanism, diagnosis, and treatment by 19 international expert authors.

confidence_components.notes: Nature Reviews Neurology consensus paper, very high source quality. Mapping fidelity moderate-high -- the broadcast-loss prediction is supported in the human knockout but the override-specifically interpretation is a translation from the multi-system clinical picture.
